exemplar.title: How do different concentrations of black tea (0%, 1%, 2%, 3%, 4%, 5%) impact the rate of digestion of proteins in Bovine Serum Albumin, when exposed to papain enzyme for 20 minutes?

detailedExemplarData.title: How do different concentrations of black tea (0%, 1%, 2%, 3%, 4%, 5%) impact the rate of digestion of proteins in Bovine Serum Albumin, when exposed to papain enzyme for 20 minutes?

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Research question situated in a detailed, discipline-specific biochemical context
Wellâjustified methodological considerations showing understanding of data sufficiency and quality
Stepâbyâstep procedural description with precise volumes and timings supports reproducibility
weaknesses: Personal narrative in introduction digresses from the core biochemical context
Insufficient detail on tea tannin preparation (mass of tea, infusion time)
Minor ambiguities in rawâdata table garble some dilution details
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The three strands were assessed at band scores of 5 (context and methodological explanation) and 4 (methodology description). The work clearly situates and explains the design, and instructions are largely reproducible. Minor omissions prevent a full 6, so a 5 reflects strong performance across all strands.

strengths: Clear presentation of processed tables and graph with error bars guiding data interpretation
Detailed spectrophotometer calibration procedure enhancing clarity
Correct statistical analysis including linear regression, r and RÂ² calculations
weaknesses: Stray symbols and missing values in raw data tables undermine precision
Uncertainty propagation is not performed, oversimplifying error treatment
Error bars based on half-range lack depth; systematic uncertainties only qualitatively addressed
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments were 3 for communication, 3 for uncertainties and 4 for processing. While data processing is accurate, presentation lacks full clarity and uncertainties are not rigorously treated. The overall performance fits the top of the 3â4 band, warranting 4.

strengths: Conclusion directly addresses the research question and aligns with numerical findings (r, RÂ², gradient)
Justification of conclusion is consistent with presented analysis
Relevant comparison to established literature on tannin inhibition
weaknesses: Discussion of percentage error impact is brief and could be more detailed
Comparison to scientific context remains at a descriptive level without quantitative literature benchmarks
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: With one strand at band 5 (relevance and consistency) and one at band 4 (scientific context), the conclusion meets the criteria for the 5â6 band overall. Minor depth limitations prevent a full 6, so a 5 is appropriate.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses are identified and their relative impacts are explained
Realistic, targeted improvements are proposed for each identified limitation
Recommendations are clearly linked to how they would mitigate the noted biases
weaknesses: Some improvement proposals lack discussion of feasibility or resource constraints
Could expand on feasibility and cost considerations for commercial reagent kits
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strands were assessed in the top band (scores of 5). The evaluation explains impacts and proposes relevant improvements. Slight generalisations in feasibility keep the criterion at 5 rather than 6.

strandSelections.reasoning: The conclusion directly answers the research question, refers to numerical outcomes (r, RÂ², gradient) and remains fully consistent with the processed data.  The justification is solid, though the discussion of percentage error reveals some small inconsistencies.  Overall the strand meets the descriptors for the 5-6 band; minor stylistic issues cap the score at 5.

Data tables, processed tables and a graph with error bars are present, so communication is clear, but precision suffers: several table cells contain stray symbols, headings are cluttered and units are occasionally omitted.  The overall layout lets the reader follow the data, yet the presentation is not simultaneously clear AND precise, so the strand sits in the 3-4 band and is awarded 3.

Means, differences, rates and a linear regression (r and RÂ²) are calculated correctly; the graph aligns with the processed table and the statistical treatment is appropriate.  Minor inaccuracies (using range/20 for error, inconsistent percentage-error table) indicate some weakness but do not constitute âsignificantâ omissions.  Thus processing is largely appropriate and accurate, awarding the top of the 3-4 band.

Four concrete methodological weaknesses are identified (timing delays, spectrophotometer drift, homemade biuret reagents, calibration issues), each followed by an explanation of how it might have affected data spread or bias.  The impact is articulated rather than merely listed, satisfying the descriptor for the top band; slight generalisation in one case prevents a perfect 6, so 5 is given.

The student links findings to established knowledge on tannin inhibition and enzyme activity, citing literature to reinforce the result.  The comparison is relevant but not deeply analytical (e.g., no quantitative literature values or mechanistic discussion beyond âcompetitive vs non-competitiveâ).  This fulfills âsome relevant comparisonâ, situating the work in the 3-4 band at 4 marks.

The student situates the research question in a detailed, discipline-specific framework.  They explain the biological role of papain and the inhibitory nature of tea tannins, with citations and a clear link to digestion, thereby providing a specific and appropriate biochemical context rather than a merely general background.  Minor digressions (personal narrative) do not detract from the overall specificity but prevent a perfect depiction, so 5 rather than 6 is awarded.

Instrument uncertainties are quoted and error bars derived from half-ranges are included; however, uncertainty propagation is not attempted, and systematic uncertainties are acknowledged only qualitatively.  Some Â± values look arbitrary (e.g., Â±0.25 mL for droppers).  Evidence of consideration is present but with notable omissions, fitting comfortably into the 3-4 band at 3 marks.

For every weakness, the student proposes a realistic, targeted improvement (more trials, fixed reading time, commercial biuret kit, single-calibration series).  Explanations show how each change would mitigate the specific limitation, moving beyond simple statements.  Minor lack of depth (no discussion of feasibility or resource constraints) keeps the score to 5 within the highest band.

Multiple methodological issuesâenzyme optimum pH/temperature, substrate choice, tannin preparation, control of volumes, buffer, timing, safetyâare discussed together with their relevance to obtaining sufficient data.  The student explains why six concentrations, five replicates and 65 Â°C were chosen, showing an understanding of how these choices affect data quality.  Some factors (e.g., justification for drop counts, replicate number) are not fully explored, so the explanation is strong but not exhaustive, meriting 5.

Step-by-step instructions with exact volumes, concentrations, times and equipment are provided, allowing almost any competent reader to reproduce the work.  Nevertheless, parts of the raw-data table are garbled, and the precise method for preparing dilutions (e.g., total volume of each solution) is sometimes implicit rather than explicit.  These minor ambiguities lower confidence in full reproducibility, placing the work at the top of the 3-4 band.

annotations.comment: The personal narrative in the introduction, while engaging, digresses from the biochemical context; consider summarizing personal experiences to maintain focus on the specific research background.
The research question is clearly stated with specific concentrations and enzyme conditions, providing a focused investigative aim.
Safety precautions for NaOH and CuSOâ are thorough, demonstrating awareness of chemical hazards.
The ethical considerations dismiss living organism concerns but overlook proper disposal of organic tea waste; add environmental disposal methods.
The raw volume table is clear, but the asterisk on 2.00 mL requires a footnote; clarify meaning directly in the header or legend.
Several stray symbols and missing values in the raw data table impede readability; ensure OCR artifacts are cleaned before analysis.
Figure 1 caption could better link pepsin activity to the papain-focused experiment to prevent confusion between enzymatic systems.
The rationale for choosing papain is well justified with literature support and relevance to the studentâs context, strengthening the experimental design.
The hypothesis is succinct and directly predicts the expected trend, providing a clear testable statement.
The hypothesis explanation could be strengthened by quantifying the anticipated rate decrease per tannin concentration increment.
The independent variable preparation lacks detail on tea bag mass and infusion time; specify brewing parameters to standardize tannin concentration.
The dependent variable is defined clearly, but consider justifying the choice of the biuret assay over other protein quantification methods.
Control of temperature is well justified with citations of denaturation and optimum activity, ensuring reproducibility.
Procedure step 2 provides precise masses and volumes for solution preparation, allowing replication with minimal ambiguity.
The spectrophotometer calibration procedure is clearly described, enhancing the clarity of data recording steps.
Step 12 mentions changing concentrations after every fifth trial but omits total replicate count; specify replicate number for statistical validity.
Error bars based on half-range oversimplify measurement uncertainty; consider using standard deviation or full propagation formula.
Graph 1 would benefit from displaying the RÂ² value and equation directly on the plot for immediate reference.
Typographical error âerror harsâ in the statistical analysis paragraph should read âerror barsâ; proofread for clarity.
The note on maintaining constant volume is a valuable clarity point, reinforcing consistent trial conditions.
Sample calculations for means are correctly applied and enhances understanding of data processing.
Uncertainties are not propagated through calculations; incorporate instrument uncertainty propagation alongside range methods.
The processed summary of rates and errors is clearly tabulated, providing concise results for comparison across concentrations.
Explanation of Pearsonâs r misses its interpretation of direction and strength; add that rââ0.97 indicates very strong negative correlation.
Limitations table identifies key methodological weaknesses with explanations, demonstrating critical evaluation.
Some proposed improvements lack feasibility discussion (e.g., cost or resources of commercial kits); explain resource considerations.
The extension suggestions are relevant and varied, offering clear pathways to broaden the investigationâs scope.
Consider adding an extension on varying enzyme-to-substrate ratios to explore kinetic parameters and inhibition constants further.
Discrepancy between trend-line intercept and observed zero-tea rate is noted; quantify how percentage error accounts for this offset.
The conclusion effectively ties RÂ² and r values to the hypothesis and data, demonstrating consistency.
Discussion of percentage error impact in the conclusion is brief; expand on how specific uncertainties affect the reliability of the negative correlation.
Linking findings to tanninâs competitive vs non-competitive inhibition mechanism would deepen the comparison to scientific context.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
The research question is stated without context.
Methodological considerations associated with collecting data relevant to the research question are stated.
The description of the methodology for collecting or selecting data lacks the detail to allow for the investigation to be reproduced.
The description of the methodology for collecting or selecting data allows for the investigation to be reproduced with few ambiguities or omissions.
The research question is outlined within a broad context.
Methodological considerations associated with collecting relevant and sufficient data to answer the research question are described.
The research question is described within a specific and appropriate context.
Methodological considerations associated with collecting relevant and sufficient data to answer the research question are explained.
The description of the methodology for collecting or selecting data allows for the investigation to be reproduced.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
The recording and processing of the data is communicated but is neither clear nor precise.
Some processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out but with major omissions, inaccuracies or inconsistencies.
The recording and processing of data shows limited evidence of the consideration of uncertainties.
The processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out but with some significant omissions, inaccuracies or inconsistencies.
The communication of the recording and processing of the data is either clear or precise.
The recording and processing of data shows evidence of a consideration of uncertainties but with some significant omissions or inaccuracies.
The processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out appropriately and accurately.
The recording and processing of data shows evidence of an appropriate consideration of uncertainties.
The communication of the recording and processing of the data is both clear and precise.

rubricCriterionStrands.name: Communication of data recording and processing

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
The conclusion makes superficial comparison to the accepted scientific context.
A conclusion is stated that is relevant to the research question but is not supported by the analysis presented.
A conclusion is described that makes some relevant comparison to the accepted scientific context.
A conclusion is described that is relevant to the research question but is not fully consistent with the analysis presented.
A conclusion is justified that is relevant to the research question and fully consistent with the analysis presented.
A conclusion is justified through relevant comparison to the accepted scientific context.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
Realistic improvements to the investigation are stated.
The report states generic methodological weaknesses or limitations.
Realistic improvements to the investigation that are relevant to the identified weaknesses or limitations, are described.
The report describes specific methodological weaknesses or limitations.
Realistic improvements to the investigation, that are relevant to the identified weaknesses or limitations, are explained.
The report explains the relative impact of specific methodological weaknesses or limitations.
